title: globularia linifolia
intro: Globularia linifolia, also known as woolly globularia, is a low-growing, perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region. Its leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Globularia linifolia can be used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues.
